data.title: Skill Nesting Observability: Trace Logging for Nested Skill Tool Invocations
data.description:
## Context
DSO practitioners running /dso:sprint on COMPLEX epics experience intermittent control flow loss when the sprint orchestrator invokes /dso:implementation-plan via the Skill tool. When this happens, the orchestrator stalls and the user must manually prompt to continue — breaking autonomous sprint flow. Today there is zero observability on Skill tool invocations (the Skill matcher was explicitly removed from hook dispatchers to reduce process count), so diagnosing the root cause is guesswork. This epic adds targeted instrumentation to the known failure paths so the next occurrence produces actionable diagnostic data.

## Success Criteria
1. Sprint orchestrator call sites that invoke /dso:implementation-plan or /dso:preplanning via Skill tool log a SKILL_INVOKE breadcrumb before invocation and a SKILL_RESUMED breadcrumb after return; on absence of SKILL_RESUMED, the orchestrator logs a CONTROL_LOSS breadcrumb (passive logging only — no automated alerting or ticket creation)
2. Child skills (implementation-plan, preplanning) log entry/exit breadcrumbs capturing: skill name, nesting depth (passed via a depth convention from the parent call site), SKILL.md file size (resolved via CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT), tool call count (session-global counter, documented as approximate), elapsed wall-clock time, session skill invocation ordinal (best-effort — resets on context compaction, documented as such), cumulative skill bytes loaded (best-effort), termination directive presence (detected by scanning the child's STATUS line only, not the full SKILL.md), and user interaction count
3. A trace analysis script parses /tmp/dso-skill-trace-*.log and produces a diagnostic report mapping each session to the 10 hypotheses (H1-H10, defined below), marking each as confirmed/refuted/insufficient-data based on defined data thresholds per hypothesis
4. Instrumentation adds zero new hook dispatcher registrations — new Bash tool calls for logging are permitted; the prohibition is on new shell processes registered in plugin.json hooks
5. The trace log format is documented in plugins/dso/docs/SKILL-TRACE-FORMAT.md and the analysis script is registered in the dso shim (.claude/scripts/dso skill-trace-analyze)
6. A synthetic validation test forces SKILL_RESUMED absence (by writing only SKILL_INVOKE to a trace log) and confirms the analysis script produces a CONTROL_LOSS entry and correctly classifies at least one hypothesis as confirmed/refuted based on the synthetic data

## Hypothesis Reference
- H1: Context compaction during child skill drops parent resume instructions
- H2: Attention dilution — too many tool results between parent call site and current position
- H3: Child skill's termination directive causes model to halt entire conversation
- H4: Large SKILL.md file size crowds out parent context
- H5: Cumulative skill load across multiple invocations exceeds threshold
- H6: Wall time / SIGURG timeout silently drops child result
- H7: Claude Code Skill tool has an undocumented nesting depth hard limit
- H8: Parent call site lacks explicit resume instruction after Skill return
- H9: Prior Skill invocation in same session corrupts state for subsequent calls
- H10: User interaction within child skill breaks the return path

Example threshold: H7 confirmed if any breadcrumb logs depth >= 3 on a session with CONTROL_LOSS; refuted if all CONTROL_LOSS sessions have depth < 3; insufficient-data if no depth field present.

## Scenario Analysis
- SKILL_RESUMED never written on abnormal child termination (high): Parent logs CONTROL_LOSS on absence regardless of cause; analysis script distinguishes STATUS:blocked from true control loss via breadcrumb sequence
- Breadcrumb Bash calls fail if /tmp not writable (critical): All echo calls use || true
- SKILL.md modifications trigger safeguard review gates (high): Expected — normal review workflow
- Analysis script must be discoverable (high): Registered in dso shim
- Session ordinal resets on /compact (high): Best-effort, documented in trace format spec
- Nesting depth undefined without convention (medium): Parent passes depth via inline convention
- Tool call count approximate (medium): Session-global counter, documented limitation
- Termination directive false positives (medium): Scan STATUS line only

## Dependencies
- 28cb-a23a (Eliminate remaining 3+ level Skill tool nesting): This observability epic should complete first — diagnostic data informs whether the nesting elimination approach is correct.

## Approach
Add inline breadcrumb logging (Bash echo ... || true to a session-scoped trace log) at the 4 implementation-plan and 3 preplanning Skill tool call sites in sprint/SKILL.md, plus entry/exit logging in implementation-plan/SKILL.md and preplanning/SKILL.md. Build a standalone analysis script registered in the dso shim. Include a synthetic validation test.

### Planning Intelligence Log

- Web research (Step 2.6): not triggered
  - Bright-line conditions that fired: none
- Scenario analysis (Step 2.75): triggered
  - Scenarios surviving blue team filter: 8
- Practitioner-requested additional cycles: none
